result.stdout:
dotnet-document:
  dotnet-document

Usage:
  dotnet-document [options] [command]

Options:
  --version         Show version information
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  apply <path>    Applies documentation on *.cs files in the [folder/solution/project].
  config          Prints the tool configuration file.

commands.payload:
dotnet-document:
  dotnet-document

Usage:
  dotnet-document [options] [command]

Options:
  --version         Show version information
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  apply <path>    Applies documentation on *.cs files in the [folder/solution/project].
  config          Prints the tool configuration file.

result.stdout:
apply:
  Applies documentation on *.cs files in the [folder/solution/project].

Usage:
  dotnet-document apply [options] [<path>]

Arguments:
  <path>    The path to a [folder/solution/project] to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory

Options:
  -c, --config <config>                                                      Set the config file path used to define documentation templates.
  -v, --verbosity <d|detailed|diag|diagnostic|m|minimal|n|normal|q|quiet>    (Default: n) Set the verbosity level. Allowed values are q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], d[etailed], and diag[nostic]
  --dry-run                                                                  Documents files without saving changes to disk. Terminates with a non-zero exit code if any files were documented.
  -?, -h, --help                                                             Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
apply:
  Applies documentation on *.cs files in the [folder/solution/project].

Usage:
  dotnet-document apply [options] [<path>]

Arguments:
  <path>    The path to a [folder/solution/project] to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory

Options:
  -c, --config <config>                                                      Set the config file path used to define documentation templates.
  -v, --verbosity <d|detailed|diag|diagnostic|m|minimal|n|normal|q|quiet>    (Default: n) Set the verbosity level. Allowed values are q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], d[etailed], and diag[nostic]
  --dry-run                                                                  Documents files without saving changes to disk. Terminates with a non-zero exit code if any files were documented.
  -?, -h, --help                                                             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
config:
  Prints the tool configuration file.

Usage:
  dotnet-document config [options]

Options:
  -c, --config <config>    Set the config file path used to define documentation templates.
  --default                Prints the default documentation config
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
config:
  Prints the tool configuration file.

Usage:
  dotnet-document config [options]

Options:
  -c, --config <config>    Set the config file path used to define documentation templates.
  --default                Prints the default documentation config
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information
